item.title: No more political appointments in financial institutions, declares finance minister
item.description: The finance minister said all future appointments would be made on professional grounds as part of a broader governance overhaul.

item.title: Minister pitches Bangladesh as ‘ethical, stable’ economy for foreign investors
item.description: “We are presenting a competitive, stable and ethical Bangladesh to you. Invest in our dream, believe us, you won’t regret it,” the LGRD minister told the audience.

item.title: PM urges every citizen to plant tree to ensure clean environment
item.description: The premier made the call after formally inaugurating a nationwide programme to plant 250 million trees by planting a garjan sapling at the Malumghat Reserved Forest in Dulahazara this afternoon.

item.title: BBS again accused of providing inflated GDP figures
item.description: BBS’s latest GDP estimates rest on a statistical record that multiple research bodies and economists say the ousted Awami League government systematically pumped up during its near-15-year rule.

item.title: Bangladesh to become South Asia’s most competitive investment market, minister pledges
item.description: The foreign minister said stability, structural reform and stronger economic diplomacy would underpin efforts to attract capital and boost competitiveness.

item.title: Public debt projected to approach BDT 34 trillion by FY29
item.description: The finance division’s recent medium‑term macroeconomic policy statement projects that over the next three years the debt pile will reach a level that squeezes the broader economy, particularly private‑sector expansion and foreign currency reserves.

item.title: US thrash Paraguay 4-1 for dream World Cup start
item.description: The emphatic Group D win marked a dream start for the co-hosts, whose four goals were their most ‌ever in a World Cup match.

item.title: UAE to unlock billions of dollars for Iran, sources say
item.description: Word of the move, which has not been previously ‌reported, coincides with the final stages of broader negotiations between Tehran and Washington on ending the war.

item.title: PM makes first Cox’s Bazar visit since taking office
item.description: Saturday’s visit is Rahman’s first to Cox’s Bazar since the BNP’s victory in the February 12 elections propelled him to the premiership.

item.title: Gold price raised to BDT 224,940 a bhori
item.description: The new rates set by the Bajus Standing Committee on Pricing and Price Monitoring took effect on Saturday.

item.title: Iran peace deal looms while new military action flares near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Draft terms of the deal described to Reuters by multiple sources indicate the U.S. would begin releasing billions of dollars in frozen Iranian assets and waive ‌sanctions on its ⁠oil exports, in return for Iran opening the strait.

item.title: Agriculture sector remains neglected as budget share fails to increase
item.description: Dr Jahangir Alam noted that compared to the overall budget increase of 18.73 percent, this rise (1.19 percent) is highly insufficient.

item.title: Large allocation in health sector, now effective implementation needed
item.description: Dr Syed Abdul Hamid said simply announcing a large budget isn’t enough; implementation is the most crucial issue.

item.title: Budget attempts to ease suffering of people and businesses
item.description: M Masrur Reaz said the tax burden hasn’t consequently increased further, and he considers this a positive step, particularly because the existing tax burden is already significant, and people are facing difficulties due to inflation.

item.title: At least 5% of budget should be allocated to education
item.description: The NSU vice-chancellor said that only 2 percent of the total budget has been allocated to education, which is highly inadequate by international standards.

item.title: Proposed budget marks positive beginning for primary textile sector
item.description: Measures aimed at protecting local industries and enhancing their competitiveness, restructuring the tariff framework, providing policy incentives for equipment supporting solar power usage, and simplifying business processes are encouraging for the primary textile sector.

item.title: Pressure on banks to increase further if revenue targets not achieved
item.description: Mashrur Arefin said that the revenue collection target appears to be highly ambitious, adding that Bangladesh has experienced significant revenue shortfalls for several years.

item.title: New Indian High Commissioner arrives in Bangladesh
item.description: Indian Deputy High Commissioner Pawan Badhe received Trivedi upon his arrival at the Benapole land port in the morning.

item.title: Sound, evidence-based planning essential for economic management
item.description: The ERG executive director noted that without institutional reforms, simplified online tax filing systems, and necessary legal changes to ensure accountability among tax administrators, such targets can’t realistically be achieved.

item.title: Jamaat says proposed budget ‘unimplementable’ and a spur to plunder
item.description: The Jamaat secretary general called on the government to amend the budget and to take effective action against what he termed disorder and irregularities already visible in the banking sector.

item.title: US-Iran peace memorandum could be signed on Sunday in Geneva, source says
item.description: Washington wants a deal to ensure that Iran never develops a nuclear weapon; Iran says it is not seeking one.

item.title: Budget sends positive signal for investment, industrialisation
item.description: The CCCI president said the budget’s emphasis on technical education will deliver effective results in the industrial sector.

item.title: Investment, employment-oriented budget key to overcoming crisis
item.description: The MGI managing director emphasised that increasing expenditure alone isn’t sufficient; equal importance must be given to whether the spending yields results and whether corruption can be controlled.

item.title: Heavy bank reliance threatens private investment, warns CPD
item.description: Dr Fahmida Khatun warned that the government’s plan to borrow BDT 1.12 trillion from the banking system to cover the shortfall would squeeze credit available to private businesses and push interest rates higher.

item.title: Economic recovery or ‘Sisyphus’?
item.description: For developing-country budgets, the Sisyphean challenge means a relentless struggle to generate growth, cut poverty and advance social development.

item.title: Finance minister says proposed budget reflects all sections of society
item.description: The finance minister said the record BDT 9.38 trillion budget for the 2026–27 fiscal year was designed to reflect the needs of all citizens.

item.title: Business-friendly, but questions remain about implementation capacity
item.description: Rubana Huq said the most important question now is whether the administrative machinery will be capable of delivering on the commitments that have been announced.

item.title: ‘Good Value for Money’ must be ensured
item.description: Dr Mustafizur Rahman emphasised that fiscal policy must remain aligned with monetary policy.

item.title: The need for qualitative transformation has been acknowledged
item.description: Dr Mustafa K Mujeri said the budget contains both a roadmap for overcoming existing challenges and a vision for future goals.

item.title: Philosophy is promising, success will depend on implementation
item.description: The real question is what measures the budget has taken to turn those principles into reality.

item.title: NASA unveils Artemis III astronauts to test technology for a future moon landing
item.description: NASA’s Artemis program aims to return astronauts to the moon’s surface for the first time since the 1970s.

item.title: Trump calls off latest threats to strike Iran, cites breakthrough in talks to end the war
item.description: Trump has said multiple times in recent weeks that the warring parties have been on the cusp of a deal without anything coming to fruition.

item.title: “The sky’s the limit,” says Mexico’s Lira after win in World Cup opener
item.description: The match, which featured three dismissals, set a record for the most red cards in an opening ​game at ​a World Cup.

item.title: Post-budget briefing set for Friday afternoon
item.description: The finance minister will attend the post-budget press conference at the Osmani Memorial Auditorium in Dhaka at 3 pm.

item.title: SpaceX IPO makes Elon Musk the world’s first trillionaire
item.description: Musk became a household name through ​Tesla and SpaceX before expanding his influence with the $44-billion acquisition of social media platform Twitter in 2022.

item.title: Iran says no final decision made on deal that Trump hopes could be signed soon
item.description: The deal, if confirmed, would be ‌the most significant diplomatic breakthrough yet to end the three-month-old war, which has killed thousands and sent global energy prices sharply higher.

item.title: Products that may become more expensive
item.description: Products facing a direct risk of price increases include cashew nuts, imported pangasius fillets, cigarettes, nicotine pouches, heated tobacco products, petrol- and diesel-powered vehicles with engine capacities ranging from 200cc to 1,600cc, imported washing machines, gypsum boards and sheets, PVC resin-based products, PET resin-based products, cold-rolled coils and sheets, copper wires and copper tubes, and polyester staple fibre, among others, according to the proposed budget.

item.title: Products likely to become cheaper
item.description: Particularly, essential commodities, electronic goods, ICT products, medical equipment, agricultural inputs, electric vehicles, dates, spices, and raw materials for various industries are expected to be directly affected.

item.title: Education, health lead Bangladesh’s record budget proposal
item.description: The finance minister presented the country’s proposed 55th national budget and the first since the BNP-led administration’s victory in the 13th parliamentary election in February this year.

item.title: Excise duty exemption limit on Bank balances raised to BDT 400,000
item.description: A new provision has also been proposed to deduct excise duty only once per year for a single loan account.

item.title: Proposed budget increases Home Ministry allocation by BDT 40 billion
item.description: The finance minister announced the allocation while presenting the FY 2026–27 budget in Parliament on Thursday afternoon.

item.title: BDT 134.65 billion budget proposed for Health Education and Family Welfare Division
item.description: The cabinet on Thursday approved the proposed national budget of BDT 9.38 trillion, which the finance minister presented before the Parliament on 3 pm.

item.title: Proposed budget includes BDT 600 billion investment stimulus
item.description: The minister said heavy borrowing in the past had intensified debt-servicing pressures, but expressed confidence of gradually narrowing the deficit.

item.title: Budget proposes BDT 1.22 trillion for three education sectors
item.description: Compared to the previous fiscal year, the allocation for the sector has increased by approximately BDT 366.29 billion.

item.title: Revenue collection target set at BDT 6.95 trillion, budget deficit at BDT 2.43 trillion
item.description: The finance minister stated that special priority has been given to social protection programmes, education, healthcare, science, research, and technology.

item.title: Budget proposes slashing withholding tax on 60 essential goods to 0.5%
item.description: “In the face of sharp price rises in recent years, this step, in line with the democratic government’s election pledge, will bring relief to people’s lives,” Chowdhury told the Parliament.

item.title: In Brazil’s Cerrado region, Indigenous fire practices reshape wildfire strategy
item.description: The Xerente live in the Cerrado, a savanna in central and northern Brazil. Every year, villages face the threat of large-scale forest fires, a danger likely to worsen with the arrival of El Niño, a phenomenon that prolongs drought and pushes regional temperatures higher.

item.title: Key priorities shaping BNP government’s first budget
item.description: It would be the largest budget in the country’s history, with the finance division indicating its size reflects pressure to deliver on election commitments

item.title: Deadly Indonesia floods wiped out at least 7% of rare orangutan population, report says
item.description: At least 58 Tapanuli orangutans, which are ​endemic to an area around north Sumatra’s Batang Toru forest, were killed in the ​floods, the report said, citing a survey of the western block of ⁠the forest that is home to a majority of the total population of 800 primates.

item.title: Cabinet clears BDT 9.38 trillion budget for FY27
item.description: The cabinet gave its approval at a special meeting chaired by Rahman at the parliament building, which began at 10:00 am and concluded at 1:00 pm.

item.title: South Korea fines Coupang $409 million in country’s largest data breach penalty
item.description: The Personal Information Protection Commission said the New York-listed company had leaked personal data of more than 33 million customers and failed to detect the breach within ​the 72 hours required by the law.

item.title: Bangladesh, China sign deal for ninth friendship bridge
item.description: A firm designated by the Chinese government will now prepare and finalise the bridge’s detailed design.

item.title: Anthropic urges US not to block state AI laws without setting federal standards
item.description: Anthropic on Wednesday weighed in on that topic, adding that ​Congress should require AI companies to put their most powerful models through independent safety tests, ​according to a company statement.

item.title: PM unveils commemorative postage stamp marking FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: The prime minister unveiled the commemorative stamp of BDT 10 at his National Parliament office this morning.

item.title: PM chairs special cabinet meeting ahead of FY27 budget
item.description: The meeting began at 10:00 am at the cabinet room in the National Parliament Building.

item.title: Bangladesh seeks stronger UNDP support for LDC transition
item.description: Reaffirming Bangladesh’s commitment to working closely with UNDP under its Strategic Plan for 2026-2029, the ambassador underscored the country’s development priorities and the importance of sustained international support during its transition from LDC status.

item.title: US launches a second day of strikes on Iran
item.description: The U.S. Central Command said its latest round of airstrikes ended just before sunrise Thursday in Iran. Iran responded by launching strikes on Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan for a second day.

item.title: North Korea, China claim wins from Xi visit, but limits remain
item.description: The two countries each lavished ‌the other with praise and spoke of deeper cooperation during the two-day trip.

item.title: ‘I love the inflation,’ Trump says as prices rise amid Iran war
item.description: Asked about U.S. government data showing consumer inflation increased at its fastest pace in three years in May, Trump said: “I love the inflation.”

item.title: GDP surpasses $500 billion as per capita income crosses $3,000
item.description: The Bangladesh Bureau of Statistics estimated GDP for FY26 at BDT 61.20 trillion — equivalent to roughly $501 billion.

item.title: Gold drops BDT 4,432 per bhori in latest cut
item.description: Successive price cuts have reduced the cost of a bhori of 22-carat gold by nearly BDT 20,000.

item.title: Once more, all roads lead to mythic Azteca
item.description: As Shakira’s familiar voice rises into the Azteca night, millions will gather before television screens—whether in a tea stall in Dhaka, a street corner in Lagos, or a neighbourhood in Buenos Aires.

item.title: Govt to launch pilot ‘e-Health Card’ programme in five districts: PM
item.description: The prime minister said that through this project, e-Health Card services will be launched within 180 days for the people of five districts — Khulna, Noakhali, Bogura, Sirajganj, and Narsingdi.

item.title: Our goal is to transform persons with disabilities into human resources: Social Welfare Minister
item.description: The minister said that the government want to empower persons with disabilities and bring them into the mainstream of society.

item.title: Per capita income reaches BDT 368,873, GDP growth at 4.14%
item.description: This is the highest per capita income recorded in Bangladesh’s history.

item.title: Eight more children die with Measles symptoms
item.description: With these latest fatalities, the total number of deaths from measles and measles-like symptoms has risen to 639.

item.title: “Bangladesh’s first recycling project for waste management to be launched in Khulna”
item.description: “Khulna City Corporation and the Asian Development Bank are jointly implementing the project with financial support from the latter. The project has an allocated budget of BDT 750 million.”

item.title: Bangladesh seeks wider EU access for skilled workers
item.description: The state minister welcomed the EU Talent Partnership initiative and urged EU member states to expand opportunities for Bangladeshi professionals and skilled workers.

item.title: Bangladesh, Germany hold talks on trade-access, infrastructure cooperation
item.description: Frank Hartmann, director-general for Asia and the Pacific at the German Federal Foreign Office, reiterated Berlin’s readiness to support Bangladesh’s energy diversification and renewable power expansion.

item.title: Parliamentary delegation to attend South Asia Cooperation Forum in Kunming
item.description: The delegation departed on a China Eastern Airlines flight at 2:00 pm.

item.title: Zubaida Rahman calls for building liveable world through tree plantation
item.description: She called for transforming tree plantation from a conventional programme into a ‘Green Revolution’ for economic, social, and generational development through public participation.

item.title: Bangladesh reaffirms commitment to BIMSTEC regional cooperation
item.description: Bangladesh, as the current Chair of BIMSTEC, remains committed to fostering trust, inclusivity and collaboration among member states, the state minister said.

item.title: Govt modernising armed forces, police for working in UN peace missions: PM
item.description: The prime minister made the remarks at a programme at Senakunja in Dhaka Cantonment this morning, marking the International Day of UN Peacekeepers.

item.title: FY27 budget due Thursday with human capital focus
item.description: Finance division officials say the budget is expected to prioritise human resource development over physical infrastructure.

item.title: Higher inland waterway charges set to ripple through supply chains
item.description: BIWTA’s revised charges for inland vessels and cargo handling after a nearly 7-year gap will take effect on July 1, adding pressure to industrial supply chains and transport costs.

item.title: Israel launched deadly strike on Lebanon’s Tyre before warning
item.description: Israel issued an evacuation warning for ​the entire city of Tyre, including its northwestern Christian quarter, at 9:31 a.m. local time (0631 GMT) through an online warning on X by an Israeli military spokesperson.

item.title: Iran targets US bases in Jordan and the Gulf after Trump orders strikes near Hormuz
item.description: The clashes mark one of the biggest exchanges in hostilities since the two countries agreed to a ceasefire in April.

item.title: Islami Bank seeks BDT 100 billion loan amid deposit flight
item.description: A senior bank executive, speaking on condition of anonymity, said the CRR deficit began last week and warned the account risked turning negative.

item.title: Gold prices cut by BDT 6,591 for 22-carat
item.description: BAJUS set the price for 22-carat gold at BDT 222,782 per bhori, effective from 10 am, citing a decrease in local market prices of pure gold.

item.title: Apple’s AI Siri will be held back by ageing devices, Morgan Stanley says
item.description: More ​than 850 million iPhones are incapable ​of running basic Apple Intelligence queries, and ⁠more than 1.3 billion iPhones cannot ​use advanced Siri features, the brokerage said.

item.title: All upazila health complexes to get 101-bed facilities: PM Info Adviser
item.description: The adviser said to expand and improve healthcare services, the government has undertaken a programme to upgrade all remaining 31-bed and 50-bed upazila health complexes to 101-bed facilities.

item.title: South Africa proposes extending auto incentives to battery materials
item.description: The government is reviewing its automotive ​policy to address the global shift toward electric and hybrid ⁠vehicles, tightening emissions rules and rising competition from low-cost imports, particularly ​from China and India.

item.title: BTV to telecast FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: In a statement issued on Tuesday, the minister said the ministry would shortly provide official details to media outlets regarding the broadcasting arrangement.

item.title: President urges South Korea to boost trade, investment with Bangladesh
item.description: The president urged the ambassador to encourage greater Korean investment in Bangladesh’s light engineering, electronics, shipbuilding, agro-processing, information technology, ready-made garments and infrastructure sectors.

item.title: President urges Morocco to hire Bangladeshi workers in healthcare, agriculture, ICT
item.description: The president stressed the importance of exchanging high-level government and private-sector visits of trade and investment delegations to further strengthen bilateral ties.

item.title: US ambassador meets Bangladesh opposition leader
item.description: Both sides discussed the post-election political and economic landscape, the upcoming national budget and bilateral trade and investment prospects.

item.title: Chief whip urges MPs’ aides to assist with budget scrutiny, strengthen democracy
item.description: He noted that MPs often trust and take advice from their assistants, and stressed the need for integrity and accountability in the role.

item.title: Three more children die with Measles symptoms
item.description: With these latest deaths, the number of children who have died with measles symptoms since March 15 has reached 539.

item.title: FRC asks 392 state entities for financial reports; only 108 complied
item.description: FRC officials believe that many of the institutions may have failed to submit reports because they had not prepared financial statements.

item.title: ECNEC clears eight projects worth BDT 22.66 billion
item.description: Three of the projects are new, three are revised, and two received tenure extensions.

item.title: No plan to drop Bangla, philosophy, history from honours courses: Edu Minister
item.description: The minister said the inclusion of new courses based on market demand or modernisation of the curriculum has no relation to excluding traditional and fundamental subjects.

item.title: Defying Trump with brief Iran fight, Israel seeks sway over peace talks
item.description: Israel and Iran both called a halt to the exchange on Monday shortly after Trump told ​them to stop shooting, although they each left the door open to a possible resumption.

item.title: US envoy pays courtesy call on PM’s Defence Adviser
item.description: Both sides discussed the excellent bilateral relations that exist between Bangladesh and the United States and various issues related to mutual interest.

item.title: Local bidders promise higher NCT revenue than DP World
item.description: Both domestic bidders claim they can out-deliver a foreign operator on revenue and ensure the strategic asset remains under national management.

item.title: Bangladesh scraps last remaining stock market floor prices
item.description: With the withdrawal, normal circuit breaker rules will apply, set under a BSEC order from June 17, 2021.

item.title: Trump says pilots are fine after US helicopter crashes near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: What caused the crash remained unclear Tuesday morning in the Middle East, which was still reeling after Iran and Israel exchanged fire the previous day in the biggest blow yet to the straining ceasefire in the Iran war.

item.title: PM chairs ECNEC meeting at Secretariat
item.description: The meeting began on Tuesday morning at the Public Administration Meeting Room of the Cabinet Division at the Bangladesh Secretariat.

item.title: Trump’s $100,000 H-1B visa fee is unlawful, US judge rules
item.description: U.S. District Judge Leo Sorokin in Boston issued the ruling in a lawsuit filed by 20 Democratic state attorneys general.

item.title: Iran and Israel say they have halted strikes on each other for now
item.description: The most direct ‌confrontation between the two countries since April threatened to wreck Washington’s efforts to reach an agreement with Tehran to end their more than three-month-old war.

item.title: Bangladesh proposes increasing manpower exports to Russia to 100,000 workers
item.description: Foreign Minister Dr Khalilur Rahman put the proposal to Russian authorities during a three-day visit to Moscow, and the Russian side agreed to expedite work on it.

item.title: Foreign minister pays tribute at tomb of unknown soldier in Moscow
item.description: During the visit, discussions are expected to cover bilateral relations between the two countries, trade and investment cooperation, energy, education, and various international and regional issues.

item.title: EU sanctions Iranians over restricting naval traffic in Hormuz
item.description: The ​move marked the first time the bloc has ​used new powers to sanction Iran for ⁠restricting freedom of navigation.

item.title: Trade deficit rises by $8 billion in five years: Commerce Minister
item.description: The minister said the country’s trade deficit has widened due to wrong policies pursued by the previous governments.

item.title: Modern water management vital for public health, environmental protection: LGRD Minister
item.description: Emphasising increased use of surface water as a sustainable solution to the long-standing water crisis in the capital, he said dependence on groundwater should be reduced, and the use of surface water increased to ensure the best utilisation of the country’s water resources.

item.title: Israel hits Iran petrochemical plant in new strikes after Trump reprimand
item.description: The escalation complicates U.S.-led efforts to broker a broader deal with Iran, driving oil prices up by nearly 5 percent, with benchmark Brent ‌futures back above $97 a barrel.

item.title: Eight more children die from measles, symptoms
item.description: The number of confirmed measles-related deaths has reached 92, while 536 children have died after showing measles-like symptoms, according to the latest data.

item.title: Bangladesh seeks Spain’s support at UNGA on LDC graduation
item.description: The two sides reaffirmed their commitment to advancing the bilateral partnership and explored avenues for expanding cooperation.

item.title: SSC, equivalent results to be published on July 20: Edu Minister
item.description: All relevant officials have received final instructions to complete evaluation and publish results simultaneously.

item.title: Earthquake of magnitude 7.8 strikes off southern Philippines
item.description: The Philippines mobilised military and disaster response teams, and authorities were verifying preliminary reports of 32 people killed and 134 ​injured across Mindanao, mostly from falling debris and landslides, according to civil defence officials.

item.title: BPDB pays nearly 40% of per-unit electricity cost as capacity charges
item.description: Under capacity charge agreements, the government is obligated to pay power companies a fixed amount regardless of whether their plants generate electricity. The charge is primarily intended to cover the costs of constructing and operating power plants.

item.title: Bangladesh UN peacekeepers’ sacrifice undervalued, says state minister
item.description: The state minister said her visits to conferences in African nations had left her convinced that the peacekeepers’ contribution is undervalued at home.

item.title: Local vaccine capability would widen Bangladesh drug exports, health minister says
item.description: The minister said Bangladesh’s pharmaceutical sector is already building a stronger position in global markets, with drugs now exported to more than 140 countries.

item.title: “LPG now a clean, safe and reliable fuel source for millions of Bangladeshi households”
item.description: “Initially, LPG was seen by many as an alternative fuel. Today, it has become the primary cooking fuel for millions of households across the country. Consumers now place greater importance on its convenience, cleanliness, safety and reliability.”

item.title: “At Petromax, safety is not just a compliance requirement but a core responsibility”
item.description: “At Petromax, we view safety not as a compliance requirement but as a core responsibility. By embedding safety into every stage of our operations — from import to end-user delivery — we aim to build trust, protect lives and ensure the sustainable growth of the LPG sector in Bangladesh.”

item.title: “United Aygaz has made the LPG market more competitive”
item.description: “Aygaz’s 65 years of global experience and international sourcing network. We believe that with the entry of United Aygaz, the market has become even more competitive. Competition keeps our competitors sharp, and their strength keeps us sharp as well. It is healthy — good for consumers, distributors and the companies themselves.”

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskiy says Russian magnate Abramovich came to Kyiv with offer of help
item.description: Abramovich, the former owner ​of Chelsea Football Club, is subject to sanctions imposed on Russia for its 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: China’s global e-commerce push stalls as Iran war lifts costs, dampens demand
item.description: Soaring logistics costs stemming from the Middle East conflict are adding to the strain, data shows and industry insiders say, with shippers like DHL Express imposing hefty fuel surcharges.

item.title: Israel hits Iran with new strikes despite Trump admonition
item.description: Hours earlier, Trump said new strikes by Israel and Iran would not affect his administration’s peace talks with Tehran, adding that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu “doesn’t call the shots.”

item.title: IGP confers rank badges on five newly promoted additional IGPs
item.description: The ceremony was held on Sunday at the Police Headquarters.

item.title: Good performance in crime control will be rewarded: Home Minister
item.description: The prime minister noted that the police had demonstrated commendable professionalism in rapidly investigating and arresting suspects in several high-profile and complex criminal cases, including the recent rape and brutal murder of Ramisa.

item.title: Tamim Iqbal elected new BCB president following board election
item.description: Former national team captain Tamim Iqbal was elected as a director with the highest tally of 73 votes and was later chosen as president.

item.title: Seven more children die with Measles symptoms
item.description: So far, 529 children have died with measles-like symptoms since March 15 across the country.

item.title: Israel kills five in Gaza as Egypt hosts new ceasefire talks
item.description: Hamas told envoys from ​the Board and mediators Egypt, Qatar and Turkey that ending Israeli attacks in Gaza was essential ​for any progress, ⁠sources from the group and officials close to the talks said.

item.title: OpenAI plans ChatGPT ‘superapp’ overhaul ahead of listing, FT reports
item.description: The overhaul will give greater prominence and resources to OpenAI’s coding product Codex and is set to roll out in the coming weeks.

item.title: South Korean president orders probe into local election ballot shortages
item.description: Last week’s South Korean local elections were hit by ⁠a ballot shortage that prevented some eligible voters from casting ballots.

item.title: US troops, families adjust to new normal of Iran war
item.description: For ​U.S. troops recovering from injuries, the military’s shift to an extended wartime footing requires a profound adjustment.

item.title: Inflation hits fiscal-year high of 9.42% in May
item.description: The figure climbed from 9.04 percent in April and stood above the 9.05 percent recorded in May last year.

item.title: Media must ensure objectivity to reflect reality accurately: Info Minister
item.description: The minister said the media is a mirror through which society sees itself, adding that if there are qualitative flaws within this system, it can’t present an accurate reflection.

item.title: Parliament opens budget session, new govt’s first fiscal plan due June 11
item.description: Finance Minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury is scheduled to table the administration’s inaugural budget for the 2026–27 fiscal year on June 11.

item.title: TIB praises some governance steps, flags lack of clear action plan
item.description: Election manifesto pledges risked remaining on paper without the urgent formulation of a national anti-corruption action plan to translate the prime minister’s stated political will into practice, Dr Iftekharuzzaman warned.

item.title: How bKash became a BDT 160 billion enterprise from a startup
item.description: Now reaching households across the most remote areas of Bangladesh, bKash serves 82 million customers. More than 47 million of them are active users who have conducted transactions within the past 90 days.

item.title: PM for skill-based, job-oriented education instead of certificate-centric learning
item.description: The current government has already started the work of making the education curriculum from primary to the highest level pragmatic and technology-based, the prime minister added.

item.title: Limited DC role slows tax base expansion
item.description: Deputy commissioners are well placed to identify new taxpayers and lift compliance through their routine work, sector insiders believe.

item.title: Couple given death sentence in Ramisa rape and murder case
item.description: Judge Masrur Salekin of the Dhaka Metropolitan Children’s Violence Suppression Tribunal delivered the verdict at around 11:45 am after finding evidence the child, Ramisa, had been raped before she was killed.

item.title: Female Navy officers say they fear a career cap after Hegseth cuts women from promotions list
item.description: The Navy had selected 31 sailors to promote from the rank of captain to one-star admiral, but Hegseth recently intervened to strike nine people from the list, including three women and two Black men, according to a defense official who spoke on condition of anonymity.

item.title: Trump faces new Republican resistance in Congress as midterm pressures build
item.description: Republican opposition on the floors of the House and Senate have been mostly symbolic to date.

item.title: US eyes Iranian assets for Gulf allies’ reconstruction, source says
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has directed a team to assess costs for damages already inflicted on Gulf allies by Iran, the source said.

item.title: Classrooms reopen across Bangladesh after 16-day holiday
item.description: Classrooms have been cleaned, lesson plans prepared and measures taken to ensure student attendance.

item.title: IDLO, BRAC University host health law moot, release South Asia toolkit
item.description: Through such events, the School of Law remains committed to providing practical learning opportunities and equipping students with the skills to become effective advocates and future leaders in the legal field.

item.title: Foreign minister, PM’s foreign adviser heads to Moscow on Sunday
item.description: The delegation is scheduled to depart Dhaka around 10 am, a source in the adviser’s office said.

item.title: Diesel price kept steady to control transportation, commodity prices: PM’s Adviser
item.description: Diesel accounts for 82 percent of total fuel consumption in Bangladesh and is used mainly by cargo trucks, public transport and agricultural irrigation.

item.title: Govt working to transform Bangladesh into trillion-dollar economy: Titumir
item.description: The roundtable was held at the ATM Shamsul Haque Auditorium of the CIRDAP International Conference Centre in Dhaka.

item.title: PM suggests environment-friendly tree plantation
item.description: The prime minister noted that the impacts of climate change are becoming increasingly evident, with noticeable changes in seasonal patterns.

item.title: Bangladesh, Turkiye to form joint ministerial committee on defence, foreign affairs

item.title: Dhaka metro extends evening service by 20 minutes from Sunday
item.description: The final train from Motijheel to Uttara will leave at 10.30 pm, and the last service from Uttara to Motijheel will depart at 9.50 pm.

item.title: 65% marginalised people exempt from electricity price hike pressure: Info Minister
item.description: The minister added that the government has adopted a long-term plan to achieve energy self-sufficiency and keep prices affordable.

item.title: BAJUS cuts gold and silver prices as pure gold costs ease
item.description: The new rates took effect at 10 am, BAJUS said, attributing the cut to a decline in the price of pure gold.

item.title: Poor waste management fuels rat boom across Dhaka, heightening health risks
item.description: Public health experts warn that a rising rodent population in the capital correlates directly with increased risks to human health. While rats may not always transmit diseases directly, they readily act as vectors for harmful pathogens.

item.title: In public letter, Ukraine’s Zelenskyy calls on Putin for direct negotiations in a neutral country
item.description: The letter, the first public message Zelenskyy has written directly to Putin since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in 2022, was a sweeping critique of the Russian leader’s 26 years in power.

item.title: UN food agency says millions are being pushed into hunger by Iran war
item.description: WFP said in its report, circulated late Thursday, that its findings indicate the Mideast crisis is generating “significant spillovers,” particularly on the prices of food and fuel, and disrupting trade.

item.title: Mobile courts to be conducted to prevent dengue, says health minister
item.description: The minister made the remarks while inaugurating a three-month special dengue prevention campaign at Rabindra Sarobar in the capital’s Dhanmondi area this morning.

item.title: Six Bangladeshi peacekeepers receive UN’s highest honour posthumously
item.description: During the ceremony, the UN Secretary-General paid tribute to peacekeepers who have lost their lives in the line of duty by laying a wreath in their honour and observing a minute of silence.

item.title: Government pushes for decision on DP World’s NCT bid
item.description: The long-running negotiations remain unsettled, and the pressure for a final call now sits squarely with the Chittagong Port Authority.

item.title: Trump’s ‘crazy’ rebuke undercuts Netanyahu at a critical moment
item.description: Netanyahu, Israel’s longest serving prime minister, has repeatedly clashed with Republican and Democratic administrations. Yet, Israel has remained Washington’s closest Middle East ally.

item.title: Salt output misses target by nearly 800,000 tonnes in 2025-26 season
item.description: Prices are climbing steadily as production trails demand by a wide margin. Mill owners and traders warn of sharper rises if supply again misses the target.

item.title: Bangladesh blocks push-in attempt; BSF takes back 17
item.description: The group was brought to the zero line near pillar 238/MP at Hapania border in Naogaon’s Sapahar sub-district around 7.30 am on Friday, according to the BGB and local residents.

item.title: Bangladesh chases third straight SAFF title against hosts India
item.description: Captain Maria Manda acknowledged India’s strength but said the squad was drawing on the experience of winning finals in Nepal in 2022 and 2024.

item.title: US attacks Iranian sites after Iran launches drones, in latest Gulf flare-up
item.description: The U.S. military believes the four Iranian drones were targeting regional maritime traffic, a U.S. official told Reuters.

item.title: Bangladesh, Türkiye eye strategic partnership, trade expansion
item.description: The foreign minister said the discussions were held in a spirit of friendship, mutual respect and understanding, reflecting the longstanding partnership between the two countries.

item.title: Only 1.5% of public university budgets allocated to research
item.description: A review of UGC annual reports shows that, despite the already modest allocation, many universities fail to utilise the full amount each year.

item.title: Attempt made to push 17 people across Naogaon border
item.description: Due to the prompt intervention of the Border Guard Bangladesh (BGB), they were unable to enter Bangladesh. The incident occurred at around 7:30 am on Friday in the Hapania border area of Sapahar Upazila.

item.title: BSF accused of attempting to push 34 people into Bangladesh through Lalmonirhat border
item.description: The attempted push-ins took place late last night. With assistance from local residents, the Border Guard Bangladesh (BGB) prevented the alleged intrusions. BGB and BSF personnel are currently positioned face-to-face along the border.

item.title: Japan could end up an ‘AI colony’ if it falls behind, digital minister warns
item.description: Matsumoto raised the warning as he ​defended a bill to amend Japan’s personal data protection law ⁠to allow AI developers to train models with data such ​as medical and criminal records without the individuals’ consent.

item.title: Nvidia CEO sees robotics as next major sector in South Korea
item.description: Nvidia CEO said Korea ​has many sectors to invest in, adding that robotics ⁠is going to be the next ​major sector here in Korea.

item.title: US House backs Russia sanctions, Ukraine aid, in latest blow to Trump
item.description: The passage came ⁠a day after a smaller group of House Republicans joined Democrats to pass a resolution that would force the withdrawal ​of troops from hostilities with Iran unless Congress declares war or orders the use of military force.

item.title: Agriculture Minister urges greater use of Biopesticides
item.description: The minister called for greater emphasis on the production and use of biopesticides, as well as wider adoption of natural pest-control methods.

item.title: Eid Travel claims 306 lives in 13 days: Road Safety Foundation
item.description: The Road Safety Foundation released the data on Thursday, citing various media reports on incidents that occurred between May 21 and June 2 during the Eid holiday period.

item.title: War as strategy: How Iran’s authoritarian regime instrumentalises conflict for domestic control
item.description: Authoritarian regimes are structurally constrained by an inherent deficit of democratic legitimacy, given the absence of free, competitive, and institutionalised electoral processes. Consequently, they must rely on alternative sources of legitimation, including nationalist narratives, ideological framing, performance-based claims, and coercive capacity.

item.title: Electricity prices rise 16.68% at consumer level
item.description: Sector experts believe the latest electricity tariff hike will have adverse effects on agricultural and industrial production, as well as on the overall living cost.

item.title: UNDP Resident Representative Stefan Liller calls on PM

item.title: PM chairs meeting on reopening, management of closed factories
item.description: The meeting was held at the Cabinet Division in the Bangladesh Secretariat this morning.

item.title: Meta repeatedly pushes back new AI model release for developers, WSJ says
item.description: A Meta spokesperson told Reuters on Wednesday ⁠that the company is already testing the ​Application Programming Interface (API) with some early partners ​and is looking forward to releasing it this month.

item.title: Israel, Lebanon agree to implement ceasefire, boosting hopes for Iran deal
item.description: The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire is contingent on a complete cessation of fire from the Iran-aligned Hezbollah militia and the evacuation of all its operatives from the South Litani Sector, a joint statement released by the U.S. State Department said following negotiations in Washington.

item.title: Oil falls as Lebanon and Israel agree on a ceasefire
item.description: Brent futures were down 87 cents, or 0.89 percent, at $96.92 a barrel by 0458 GMT, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude fell 78 cents, or 0.81 percent, to $95.24, paring gains from earlier in the week.

item.description: In this role, he will focus on driving innovation, setting strategic priorities, and steering organisational transformation to ensure sustainable growth and future readiness.

item.title: PM chairs meeting on DND dam to find ways to resolve waterlogging
item.description: He said the meeting discussed in detail the long-standing water-logging problem in the DND embankment area and possible ways to resolve it.

item.description: Bangladesh Women’s Football Team secured its third consecutive SAFF Women’s Championship Final.

item.title: Budget to be placed considering every citizen of Bangladesh: Finance Minister
item.description: The minister said that the government has been working on restoring sound public financial management to prevent the country from falling into a debt trap in the coming days, as a huge amount is now being spent to repay the interests of loans in the national budget.

item.title: Govt taking steps to fulfil election pledges: LGRD Minister
item.description: The minister said the government has taken initiatives to establish Thakurgaon University, Thakurgaon Medical College and an Engineering College, reopen the district airport and set up an Export Processing Zone (EPZ) to accelerate the area’s socio-economic development.

item.description: The minister called for increased institutional investment in education, technological cooperation, vocational training, and research between the two countries.

item.title: Default loans rise by BDT 314.87 billion in first quarter of the year
item.description: Outstanding default loans in the banking sector exceeded approximately BDT 5.88 trillion at the end of March, accounting for 32.26 percent of total outstanding loans, according to Bangladesh Bank data.

item.title: Two Bangladeshis injured by BSF gunfire along Satkhira border
item.description: The two men had travelled to India “illegally” from Bangladesh about a year ago, according to police and local sources. They had since been working as construction labourers in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu after travelling through Howrah in Kolkata. They were reportedly attempting to return to Bangladesh illegally on the night of the incident.

item.title: Japan’s Mitsui delegation calls on PM
item.description: During the meeting, the two sides discussed investment opportunities and the expansion of investment in various sectors, including food, agriculture, energy and ICT.

item.title: Google must let UK publishers opt out of AI search under new rules
item.description: The country’s Competition and Markets Authority has flagged concerns about Google’s dominance in search, designating the company with ​the “strategic market status” that allows it to set targeted rules to increase trust and ​transparency.

item.title: US Proposes additional tariffs on goods from Bangladesh and 59 other economies
item.description: The proposal from the U.S. Trade Representative’s office is the latest finding from a Section 301 unfair trade practices investigation to be released as the Trump administration seeks to ​rebuild its emergency tariffs, which were struck down by a U.S. Supreme Court decision in ​February.

item.title: US sanctions Iran’s largest crypto exchange over IRGC links
item.description: The new sanctions follow a Reuters investigation published on May 1, which showed how Nobitex had become a central node in a parallel financial system used to process hundreds of millions of ​dollars for Iran’s central bank and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

item.title: Govt announces major legal reforms, heightened border security measures: Home Minister
item.description: The minister said a new law is being drafted to replace the antiquated 1867 Gambling Act, with plans to present it in the upcoming parliamentary session.

item.title: UN committee backs Bangladesh’s request for extension of LDC graduation period
item.description: CDP Chair Professor José Antonio Ocampo emphasised that Bangladesh would need to make significant progress in implementing key domestic reforms to address its existing structural vulnerabilities during this extended period.

item.title: Upcoming budget to focus on empowering poor, marginalised people: Finance Minister
item.description: The finance minister said the low-income people have historically been the most deprived in Bangladesh’s budgetary framework, adding that the government has therefore given priority to the poor, low-income groups, and homemakers (housewives) in the upcoming budget.

item.title: Operating expenditure accounted for 84% of government spending
item.description: A significant portion of the operating budget was spent on salaries and allowances for government employees, pensions, subsidies, and interest payments on loans.

item.title: Lebanon announces partial ceasefire between Israel, Hezbollah but attacks continue
item.description: According to Lebanon’s embassy in Washington, the agreement would not end the conflict in that country. But it calls for Israel to refrain from strikes on Beirut and its suburbs controlled by Hezbollah, while the Iran-aligned group would halt its attacks on Israel.

item.title: Nvidia has capacity to supply robust AI growth despite constraints, says CEO
item.description: The company, considered a barometer for the AI market’s health as its semiconductors are used in virtually every major data centre in the world, acknowledged, however, that supply constraints remain a ​concern.

item.title: STMicroelectronics raises data centre revenue target on AI demand
item.description: The Franco-Italian chipmaker now ​expects data centre revenue of about $1 ​billion in 2026, compared with its previous forecast for revenue “nicely above” $500 million.

item.title: Gold price falls by BDT 3,266 per bhori
item.description: As in previous adjustments, the association said in the statement that the price of pure gold in the local market has declined. Considering the overall market situation, new gold prices have therefore been fixed.

item.title: Russian drones, missiles strike Ukraine in major attack; 11 dead, scores wounded
item.description: Russia has targeted Ukraine’s power supply and infrastructure in a war now more than four years old, while Ukraine has stepped up attacks this year on Russian oil facilities. Both deny targeting civilians.

item.title: Remittance inflow rises 15.34% to $3.43 billion in May
item.description: The country received $3.43 billion in remittances during May 2026 against $2.97 billion in May 2025, reflecting the continued confidence of expatriate Bangladeshis in using formal banking channels to send money home.

item.title: Tofail Ahmed is no more
item.description: In a statement, the hospital authorities said that the politician had long been suffering from various age-related complications. On September 24, he was admitted to Square Hospital due to pneumonia-related respiratory distress, heart disease, and physical weakness. He had been on life support there.

item.title: Entrepreneurs and businesses concerned over NBR’s tax plans
item.description: Entrepreneurs and businesses fear that the government may ultimately seek to address the substantial revenue shortfall by placing additional burdens on existing taxpayers instead of broadening the tax base by bringing new taxpayers into the system.

item.description: Dipen Dewan cited physical illness as the reason for his resignation, according to sources from the Prime Minister’s Press Wing.

item.title: Trial begins in Ramisa murder case, testimony hearing set for tomorrow
item.description: The two accused were brought to court in a prison van at around 7:45 am. Shortly before 11:00 am, Sohel Rana was produced before the court under tight police security. About ten minutes later, Swapna Akter was also brought before the tribunal.

item.title: China tightens overseas investment rules after blocking Meta-Manus deal
item.description: The regulations provide ​for the first time a comprehensive and formalised legal basis for China to force the unwinding of completed overseas ​transactions — heightening compliance risks for global investors in sensitive sectors like Chinese tech and AI.

item.title: Oil rises as US and Iran trade strikes, Israel moves further into Lebanon
item.description: The stepped-up fighting, coming just after the U.S. hosted Israel-Lebanon peace talks in Washington ​on Friday, dimmed expectations that the U.S. and Iran could soon announce an extension to their ceasefire agreement, which ​had driven Brent and WTI to settle down 1.8 percent and 1.7 percent, respectively, on Friday.

item.description: The U.S. and Iran have sporadically exchanged strikes since their ceasefire took effect in early April as diplomacy aimed at a more durable agreement drags on.

item.description: Tourists from different parts of the country are exploring the district’s popular attractions, while many are taking engine-powered boats to tour Kaptai Lake.

item.description: A large portion of the hides arriving earlier in the day were fresh, blood-stained raw hides. After reaching the tanneries, workers quickly applied salt to preserve them.

item.description: Dr Abdul Moyeen Khan made these remarks in an interview with BSS on the eve of the 45th martyrdom anniversary of Ziaur Rahman, observed on May 30.

item.description: Public health specialists note that this year’s weather conditions have sharpened the threat. Repeated rainfall interspersed with warmer spells has sped up mosquito breeding.

item.description: Hegseth, who was with Trump in Beijing, said the two leaders had agreed that China and the U.S. should “build a constructive relationship of strategic stability.”

item.title: Myanmar’s junta chief turned president heads to India, with an eye on China
item.description: The five-day trip, during which the former general will hold talks with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, underscores the gradual return of regional re-engagement for Myanmar.

item.description: While much of Iran’s uranium enrichment infrastructure was destroyed or badly damaged when Israel and the U.S. bombed ​it in June, a large part of the highly enriched uranium it amassed is thought to have survived.

item.description: Trump is under pressure to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and get U.S. gasoline prices down ahead of the November congressional elections, as voters show increasing frustration over rising prices.

item.description: Blue Origin ​was preparing the rocket for its fourth launch, which was due to deliver 48 Amazon Leo satellites into low-Earth orbit, part of efforts ​to build a broadband constellation to rival Musk’s Starlink network.

item.description: Sudan Doctors Network, a group that tracks violence across the country, blamed forces affiliated with the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces for carrying out the attacks.

item.description: Russia’s warning to carry out “consistent and systematic” missile strikes on Kyiv, accompanied by a call for evacuating foreign embassies from the capital, signals Putin’s intention to expand Russia’s barrage despite the heavy costs and potential international outrage.

item.title: Ward where six newborns died lacked ventilation when AC was off, says Health DG
item.description: Director General Professor Pravath Chandra Biswas told reporters after inspecting Ad-Din Medical College Hospital in Dhaka’s Mogbazar area that the room had been left with no alternative air supply.

item.description: Her climb follows the ascents of Nishat Mazumder on May 19, 2012 and Wasfia Nazreen on May 26, 2012, the first two Bangladeshi women to conquer Everest.

item.description: Since March 15, a total of 88 people have died from confirmed measles and 467 from suspected measles.

item.title: Rain hits Dhaka livestock markets during peak Eid trading
item.description: A downpour that began around midday in Dhaka and lasted up to two hours left market grounds waterlogged, forcing a large share of buyers to return empty-handed and leaving traders struggling to shelter their animals.

item.title: Minister says Eid animal trade now key income source for marginal farmers
item.description: Farmers and livestock rearers raise cattle, goats, sheep and buffalo for one to two years before bringing them to market for Eid-ul-Adha, creating a critical economic window for smallholders, the livestock minister said.

item.title: DSCC ready to remove sacrificial waste within eight hours
item.description: Around 174,406 animals are expected to be sacrificed within DSCC areas this year.

item.title: Power Division assures uninterrupted electricity supply during Eid
item.description: The Power Division has said it has ensured round-the-clock monitoring of power generation, transmission and distribution systems, while emergency repair teams have been kept on standby to respond quickly to any disruption.

item.title: Khulna’s Sheikhpara traders forced into open-air hide market
item.description: The area, known locally as Sheikhpara Chamrapatti, once housed about 10 shops and 35 traders. Most of those shops have now closed.

item.title: Eid-ul-Adha congregation at Parliament Building South Plaza at 7:30 am
item.description: All concerned have been requested to arrive on time to attend the Eid congregation.

item.title: Bogura hide traders head into Eid season with BDT 220 million unpaid dues
item.description: More than 200 traders routinely borrow ahead of the festival to purchase hides and salt, relying on full payment from Dhaka-based tanneries to recover their capital.

item.title: Ready-made tools blunt demand for Mirsarai’s traditional blacksmiths
item.description: In the old blacksmith quarters of markets such as Mithachhara Bazar, the ring of hammer on anvil still rises with the festival season, but craftsmen report that rising iron and coal prices have cut their margins while customers increasingly shun bespoke work.

item.title: BDT 250 billion highway upgrade fails to ease Eid gridlock on northern route
item.description: Experts blame the protracted upgrade works as a principal cause of congestion in the Dhaka–Tangail artery. Sprawling roadside markets and mismanagement only compound the gridlock.

item.title: Israel’s military says it’s striking Hezbollah sites as Netanyahu vows to ‘increase the blows’
item.description: Netanyahu’s warning came as Hezbollah has been firing fiber optic drones at Israeli forces in southern Lebanon and northern Israel in recent weeks.

item.title: Brent oil gains 2 percent as US military strikes on Iran add to uncertainty on potential peace deal
item.description: Brent futures were up $1.98, or 2.1 percent, to $98.12 a barrel as of 0405 GMT, after settling 7 percent lower in the previous session.

item.title: Rubio says Iran deal could take days as US launches fresh strikes
item.description: Describing the strikes against targets including boats attempting to lay mines ‌and missile launch sites, Rubio said the Strait of Hormuz has to be open “one way or the other.”

item.title: Experts discuss opportunities, risks in evolving US–Bangladesh trade ties
item.description: The discussion examined the implications of the recent US–Bangladesh trade agreement, its possible effects on major sectors of the Bangladeshi economy, and the broader strategic, regulatory, and geopolitical questions surrounding the agreement.

item.title: Fire breaks out at Kalshi slum in capital, four Fire Service units deployed
item.description: Three additional units from the Mirpur and Kurmitola fire stations are also being sent to assist.

item.title: Trump and Warsh’s fates are now tied, for better or worse
item.description: With new Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh now installed as the country’s central ​bank chief — completing Trump’s imprimatur across the top echelon of U.S. economic policymaking posts — the dynamic shifts.

item.title: Rethinking fiscal governance: A blueprint for Bangladesh’s tax future
item.description: Fiscal reform in Bangladesh must go beyond revenue targets. It is not enough to chase numbers; the system must be rebuilt to foster trust and voluntary compliance. By embracing transparency, democracy, prestige, agility, and empathy, the country can unlock a new wave of civic participation.

item.title: Indian finance minister calls for focus on ‘3Fs’ – fuel, fertiliser, forex
item.description: The prime minister ​giving a call to conserve foreign exchange, as far ​as possible, is very important, Sitharaman said, adding that the stress on 3Fs – fuel, fertiliser, and foreign exchange – should be viewed ​in this context.

item.title: Raw cotton imports through Bhomra Land Port fall by nearly 40%
item.description: Traders and importers said imports have decreased because of lower demand from local garment factories and textile mills producing yarn.

item.title: ADB unveils $5 billion financing for Bangladesh over five years amid cost pressures
item.description: During a visit to Dhaka, ADB President Masato Kanda met the prime minister, finance minister, and private sector leaders, pledging expanded support for reforms, financing gaps, energy diversification and private investment mobilisation.

item.title: Measles and its symptoms claim 17 more lives in 24hrs
item.description: DGHS on Monday disclosed the information in the measles-related report.

item.title: President to host Eid-ul-Adha reception at Bangabhaban
item.description: The president and his spouse will exchange greetings with distinguished personalities and people from various classes and professions.

item.title: ADB President calls on PM
item.description: The meeting was held at the Prime Minister’s Office in Tejgaon in the capital this morning.

item.title: Bangladesh farmland faces twin threat of acidity and depleted soil quality
item.description: Soil scientists say that an increase in soil acidity directly affects agricultural production. In acidic soil, normal root growth is hindered, and plants cannot properly absorb essential nutrients, they added.

item.title: RAB camp in Jungle Salimpur attacked; infrastructure demolished with bulldozers
item.description: During the attack, bulldozers were used to demolish the camp’s walls and other infrastructure. Roads were also dug up at least three locations in an attempt to obstruct operations by law enforcement agencies.

item.title: Defaults deter banks from fresh lending to leather sector
item.description: The livestock ministry projects that more than 10 million cattle, buffaloes, goats and sheep will be sacrificed. The loan target for preserving these hides is just BDT 2.28 billion, but industry insiders warn actual disbursements could slip below BDT 1 billion, as they did last year.

item.title: Trump says not to rush as details emerge of a potential Iran deal
item.description: Trump said negotiations were “proceeding in an orderly and constructive manner,” and the relationship with Iran was becoming “much more professional and productive.”

item.title: DNCC deploys 16,000 workers for Eid waste management
item.description: According to DNCC, around 6,500 sanitation workers, 4,500 household waste collectors and nearly 5,500 volunteers will work in the field to ensure swift removal of sacrificial waste from Eid day.

item.title: BAJUS lifts gold, silver prices as domestic pure gold costs climb
item.description: The BAJUS Standing Committee on Pricing and Price Monitoring set the new rates at a meeting on Monday morning.

item.title: Bangladesh offers export rights, tax breaks in offshore energy push
item.description: The move follows a failed 2024 offshore round in which no bids were submitted after investors raised concerns over pricing, tariffs, data availability and labour-related costs. Those issues were reviewed over the past year, leading to a redesigned contract framework and updated terms

item.title: At least 15 dead as truck overturns near Jamuna Bridge
item.description: The vehicle, bound for northern Bangladesh from Dhaka, lost control and flipped on the highway, killing 15 at the scene.

item.title: China sends astronaut on year-long space mission as it eyes 2030 moon landing
item.description: The Shenzhou-23 flight will execute the first autonomous rapid rendezvous and docking procedure with the core module of Tiangong in preparation for the 2030 mission.

item.title: Vessels carrying Middle East oil, LNG exit Hormuz, head for Pakistan, China
item.description: The vessels ​are among a handful of supertankers exiting the Gulf this month via a transit route ⁠that Iran has ordered ships to use.

item.title: Oil slips 6% as US, Iran seen moving closer to peace deal
item.description: Brent crude futures fell $5.85, or 5.7 percent, to $97.69 a barrel, while U.S. West ​Texas Intermediate were at $90.85 a barrel, down $5.75, or 6 percent.

item.title: Central bank announces BDT600B stimulus package to reopen factories, create jobs, revive economy
item.description: The package, titled “Private Sector Development, Employment Generation and Economic Revival,” aims primarily to revitalise the country’s economy. It emphasises reopening closed industrial units, creating new employment opportunities, strengthening the agriculture and export sectors, and increasing the flow of funds into the private sector to restore momentum in overall economic activities.

item.title: On first official India trip, Rubio tries to halt a trust deficit between Washington and Delhi
item.description: Rubio arrived Saturday on his first official visit to India ahead of a meeting set for Tuesday with his counterparts from India, Australia and Japan, which are members of the Indo-Pacific strategic alliance known as the Quad.

item.title: Beyond relief: rethinking the Rohingya response
item.description: The Rohingya response is no longer only a humanitarian challenge. It is also a question of long-term policy, regional stability, and shared responsibility. Continuing to meet immediate needs remains essential, but it must be complemented by forward-looking approaches that expand opportunities and reduce dependency over time.

item.title: Why democracy so often slips away after an uprising and what it takes to hold on
item.description: Democracy is not born in a single moment of uprising. It is built or lost in what comes after.

item.title: Dhaka, Delhi divided over water-sharing basis as Ganges treaty nears expiry
item.description: Neither government has yet said whether the existing pact will be renewed or replaced, but their respective ministries confirm that technical teams from both sides have begun preliminary work.

item.title: Thomas Dooley appointed new head coach of Bangladesh national football team
item.description: BFF is hopeful and thrilled to appoint American-origin German Thomas Dooley as the new head coach of the Bangladesh national football team.

item.title: Korea Post eyes AI data centres, real estate investment for higher returns amid mail losses
item.description: Korea Post is still cautious on office ​buildings, but is looking at so-called secondary funds investing in data centres, logistics facilities, and multi-family houses in North America and ​Europe.

item.title: Samsung’s deal with union hailed as a victory as bonuses less generous than SK Hynix’s
item.description: Samsung workers had been furious about the large gap in bonuses with their peers at smaller rival SK Hynix, which had beaten Samsung to the punch ​in delivering advanced AI chip units to Nvidia, bolstering its fortunes.

item.title: Ammonium, phosphate levels in Karnaphuli several times higher than normal
item.description: The critical condition poses a serious threat to aquatic life, fish breeding, and the overall ecological balance of the river.

item.title: The worst climate future is less likely, but the best one is slipping away, scientists say
item.description: Researchers’ new list of seven plausible carbon pollution scenarios for the future are pushing aside two staples of climate policy: the extremes on either end.

item.title: SpaceX IPO filing lays bare losses and Musk control as it stakes future on AI
item.description: For many, Musk’s record turning Tesla into the most ‌valuable auto company in the world and developing the world’s first fully reusable rocket and largest satellite network is enough to justify investment.

item.title: SpaceX’s upgraded Starship V3 ready for debut launch ahead of IPO
item.description: The towering spacecraft, ⁠consisting of the upper-stage Starship astronaut vessel stacked atop its Super Heavy booster rocket, was due for launch as early as Thursday, the company said late on Monday.

item.title: At least 18 killed as heavy rains drench southern, central China
item.description: China’s weather agency said areas of Jiangxi, Anhui, Hunan, Hubei, Guizhou, Guangxi, Guangdong and Hainan faced a ​high risk of rain-related disasters, including landslides, flash floods and severe urban flooding, and waterlogging.

item.title: ‘Actuary’ in insurance industry and its importance, current status in Bangladesh
item.description: Actuarial science is a highly respected profession and offers both prestige and substantial financial rewards. Unfortunately, in Bangladesh, the actuarial profession has not yet developed adequately.

item.title: Bangladesh secure ‘hat-trick’ Test win over Pakistan
item.description: With the victory, the side led by Najmul Hossain Shanto took a 1-0 lead in the two-match Test series.

item.title: “A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation”
item.description: “The fundamental weakness is that we still treat the budget essentially as an income-expenditure ledger. A national budget should serve as a policy document for economic transformation. It should spell out which sectors the government will prioritise over the coming year…”

item.title: “Upcoming budget will include every individual and community”
item.description: “In the past, many groups remained outside the scope of the budget, with no dedicated projects for them. This time, we will bring them under inclusion. We have spoken about the ’democratisation of the economy’ — this is our slogan.”

item.title: GolfHouse Diplomatic Cup 2026 concludes in Dhaka with diplomats, corporate leaders participating
item.description: Organisers said the event is part of a broader initiative to integrate golf into corporate culture and strengthen engagement between diplomats and business leaders through informal interaction, emphasising networking, discipline and leadership.

item.title: “Priority must go to dignified livelihood and safety for migrants abroad”
item.description: “We have to put human dignity first. Our human rights infrastructure needs to be strengthened. We’ve got to make sure our workers can earn a dignified living and stay safe while they’re abroad.”

item.title: Runa Laila to receive ‘Minar-E-Dilli’ honour
item.description: The honour will be presented to her at the 15th Delhi International Film Festival in Delhi.

item.title: Second T20I abandoned due to rain
item.description: The third and final match of the series is scheduled for May 2 at Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium, starting at 2:00 pm.

item.title: “Strong possibility of a food crisis if Hormuz issue not resolved”
item.description: “Thirty-four percent of the world’s fertilisers come through the Strait of Hormuz. Due to the blockage, there is a strong possibility of a food crisis in the coming months if this issue is not resolved.”

item.title: We can bounce back, says Shariful Islam
item.description: Pace bowler Shariful Islam, who returned figures of two for 27 from his 10 overs in the first match, insisted the side can bounce back.

item.title: Asha Bhosle, one of India’s most versatile Bollywood singers, passes away at 92
item.description: She was admitted to the hospital on late Saturday with a chest infection and exhaustion, her granddaughter Zanai Bhosle said in a social media post.

item.title: BCB board dissolved, Tamim Iqbal to lead ad hoc committee
item.description: The council informed the International Cricket Council of the dissolution by email and has submitted the ad hoc committee’s name for approval.

item.title: An extraordinary story of survival retold on screen
item.description: Each day brought uncertainty and the film brings that experience to the big screen.

item.title: “We must continue to push for regional cooperation, increase people-to-people dialogue”
item.description: “As residents of South Asia, we must continue to push for regional cooperation and increase people-to-people dialogue. This is essential for trade connectivity. For instance, the South Asian Free Trade Agreement (SAFTA) exists, but it has not been able to work effectively due to logistical and paperwork constraints.”

item.title: Bangladesh archery team accorded warm reception after Asia Cup gold
item.description: The youth and sports secretary welcomed the team with floral wreaths. He extended his heartfelt congratulations to the players, coaches, and officials for their outstanding achievement.

item.title: A major milestone for Bangladesh’s cinema industry
item.description: Competing against 11 other features in the Big Screen Competition at the International Film Festival Rotterdam (IFFR), the Bangladeshi film “Master” has earned its place among the world’s most prestigious film festivals.

item.title: Bangladeshi folk film ‘Behula Dorodi’ to screen at international festivals
item.description: Jahidul Islam, the producer, said he aims to establish Bengali cinema as a viable commercial proposition internationally while preserving “dying folk traditions” for a new generation through the film.

item.title: Warner Bros rejects Paramount’s revised offer, sets 7-day deadline for better bid
item.description: Paramount informally broached an even higher share price, $31 a share, Warner Bros said, apparently enticing the board to the table. However, its response to Paramount still makes it clear that Warner Bros prefers the Netflix deal, and the odds of a switch are long.
